evals.name: check inbox and reply
evals.prompt: A user says: check whether any other agents messaged me and reply with my status. Describe the ccgram workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses ccgram inbox/status workflow, reads messages, identifies peers/session, replies or broadcasts status, and does not invent messages.
assertions: The output treats this as inter-agent ccgram messaging.
The output checks inbox or pending messages before replying.
The output identifies the relevant peer, session, or channel before sending.
The output sends a reply or broadcast with concise status as requested.
The output avoids inventing messages if the inbox is empty.

evals.name: spawn or discover peer
evals.prompt: A user asks to coordinate with another agent working in the same tmux session. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response discovers peers or spawns/contacts agents through ccgram, sends scoped messages, checks for replies, and reports coordination status.
assertions: The output discovers existing peers or agents before assuming one exists.
The output uses ccgram send/broadcast or equivalent swarm messaging.
The output scopes the message with the task and needed response.
The output checks or waits for replies when coordination matters.
The output reports what was sent and any response/status.
